text: Amidst the ongoing wave of digital transformation, blockchain technology has emerged as a robust framework for addressing systemic issues surrounding interoperability, data privacy, and resource optimization. By providing a decentralized architecture, this technology holds the potential to fundamentally revolutionize modern healthcare delivery. This study investigates the transformative capacity of blockchain within the medical sector, exploring how it serves as a strategic catalyst for overcoming long-standing structural obstacles. Adhering to the PRISMA methodology, we conducted a systematic review of ten pivotal studies centered on the design and execution of blockchain-based solutions. Our analysis highlights critical applications in data security, pharmaceutical supply chain integrity, and cross-institutional data sharing. Ultimately, this paper provides a comprehensive synthesis of the current landscape of blockchain in healthcare, weighing its substantial clinical and administrative benefits against the inherent challenges of large-scale implementation.
